untranslatable_reason: Signal requires order book microstructure data (limit order presence, bid-ask spread, order cancellation events) which are not available in the standard OHLCV indicator set. The core logic depends on: (1) detecting limit orders on book, (2) measuring deviation from traded prices, (3) monitoring order cancellations, and (4) real-time bid-ask spreads. These require tick-level order book data, not candle-based indicators.
translation_notes: This is a high-frequency microstructure signal that exploits limit order provider free options. It cannot be accurately translated using only OHLCV-derived indicators. A proper implementation would require: order_book depth, limit_order_flag, order_cancellation_event, bid_ask_spread, and tick-level timestamps. The attempted translation uses price deviation (atr_14 as volatility proxy) and returns thresholds, but this fundamentally misses the signal's true logic of identifying and trading against specific limit order placements.
